summary: Iteration 24 full-stack verification of (1) GET /api/footfall/weekday-pattern and (2) the new Data Quality console at /data-quality. All acceptance criteria green on backend + frontend. 7/7 pytest cases pass. UI end-to-end flow verified: heatmap renders 15×7=105 cells above the existing charts, mode toggle repaints, narrative line is correct; DQ page shows both expected flags (Shop Zetu 16.09% return rate WARN 5.2σ, Vivo Junction 52.50% CR SEVERE 4.8σ), Mark-PO-raised flow fires toast + hides row + increments 'already investigated' counter + persists across reload via recommendation_state collection with item_type='dq'; empty state copy matches spec; How-it-works card states actual group mean + sd.
verified_features: GET /api/footfall/weekday-pattern requires auth — 401 without Bearer token ✓
Default 28-day window with {start, end, days=28}; rows sorted desc by total_footfall_window; each row has by_weekday length 7; every weekday has exactly 4 days of samples ✓
Cache: second call returns in <2s (measured) vs ~7s cold call ✓
Recommendations item_type='dq' POST→GET→DELETE round-trip works; 'reorder' and 'ibt' still return 200; bogus item_type returns 422 ✓

Frontend /footfall: [data-testid='weekday-heatmap'] present, 105 wkd-cell-<Store>-<Day> elements (15 stores × 7 weekdays), narrative 'Group peak: Sat (3,152 avg). Softest: Sun. Best CR day: Fri (13.4%)' matches spec, mode toggles heatmap-mode-footfall / heatmap-mode-conversion repaint cells, legend 'Quiet day … Peak day … Dashed cell = no data' present ✓
DOM order: heatmap Y=479 < first recharts-wrapper Y=1226 → heatmap ABOVE per-day charts ✓

Frontend /data-quality: [data-testid='data-quality-page'], h1 'Data Quality', 4 KPI tiles (dq-kpi-total 'ACTIVE FLAGS 2 all pending', dq-kpi-severe 1, dq-kpi-warn 1, dq-kpi-metrics 2 with 'Learn how it works' pill) ✓

dq-flags-table shows exactly 2 expected flags in This Month window: Warn · Online - Shop Zetu · Return rate · 16.09% · 1.75% · 5.2σ · 'Above 2σ of group avg (1.7%) — verify the return rate source.' · 'KES 6,092,894 sales · KES 980,555 returns' · Pending · Change; and Severe · Vivo Junction · Conversion · 52.50% · 14.43% · 4.8σ · 'Unusually high CR (≥50%) — likely counter miscalibration' · '831 visitors sampled' · Pending · Change ✓

Action pill: clicking Change → Mark PO raised fires green toast 'PO raised · conversion_rate::Vivo Junction' (and 'return_rate::Online - Shop Zetu'); row hides from default view; Active Flags KPI decrements and subtitle flips to 'N already investigated' ✓
Empty state after both marked: '🎉 Every open flag has been investigated. Toggle "Show resolved" to review.' — exact copy match ✓
dq-show-resolved toggle present; count updates as flags are resolved (Show resolved (2)) ✓
State persists across hard reload — confirmed via GET /api/recommendations?item_type=dq returning both POSTed rows with status 'po_raised' after reload ✓

How-it-works card [data-testid='dq-how'] below table: 'Over physical stores with ≥ 200 visitors, we compute the group mean (14.43%) and standard deviation (7.94%pp)' + 'Over stores with ≥ KES 100k sales, same 2σ math. Group mean 1.75% ± 2.76%pp.' — actual group stats wired in ✓
Sidebar: new 'Data Quality' tab with ⚠ icon sits between CEO Report and Exports ✓

Regression: existing outlier-banner/outlier-pill logic still on Footfall page (verified present when window has data; on Today window both are expectedly absent because 0 orders that day — not a regression)
critical_code_review_comments: server.py line 1226: group_avg_by_weekday avg_footfall uses sum(s[0])/max(1, len(set of days)) where the set iterates results and does the weekday filter inside the len() — O(7×28) on a hot path but acceptable; the expression is nested and hard to read. Consider pre-bucketing day→results once before the loop.

/api/footfall/weekday-pattern cache is in-memory per process and keyed only on (start, end, country). It will grow unbounded if called with many custom ranges. Consider an LRU cap (cachetools.TTLCache) or evicting on add.
server.py is 2324 lines — well over the 700-line guideline. Splitting the endpoint blocks (footfall, sales, recommendations routing) into separate modules is overdue.

Weekday pattern relies on upstream returning a `location` string per daily row; if upstream ever changes that field name the endpoint will silently return an empty rows[] list (fan-out succeeds but aggregation produces nothing). A health probe returning counts per day would surface this early.

DQ page wires item_type='dq' into the shared recommendation_state collection — good reuse, but note that the Pydantic Literal enforces status ∈ {pending, po_raised, dismissed, done}. If future DQ flows want a dedicated 'investigated' status, the whole enum needs updating — current UI works around this by using 'po_raised' to mean investigated, which is slightly overloaded semantically (reorder domain term leaking into DQ).
test_credentials: admin@vivofashiongroup.com / VivoAdmin!2026 (from /app/memory/test_credentials.md). Direct POST /api/auth/login works from in-cluster (pytest) but returns 403 from the browser-automation egress; in the browser flow the email login form works.
seed_data_creation: Two dq recommendation_state rows were POSTed during UI flow testing (conversion_rate::Vivo Junction and return_rate::Online - Shop Zetu, both status=po_raised). Both were cleaned up via DELETE /api/recommendations?item_type=dq&item_key=... at end of run (verified empty).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter 24 verified Weekday-pattern endpoint + /data-quality page green. Backend test suite at /app/backend/tests/test_iteration_24_weekday_dq.py exercises the auth gate, shape, 4-samples-per-weekday invariant, cache (<2s), invalid dates, dq POST/GET/DELETE, regression on reorder/ibt, and rejection of unknown item_type. Next iterations: the heatmap DOM order check assumes .recharts-wrapper classes exist under Footfall — if footfall charts ever switch away from recharts, update the selector. Direct /api/auth/login returns 403 from the browser-automation IP but the email login form works — use the form for any UI-driven test.
rca of the issue: No bugs found. Both shippable features (weekday heatmap + /data-quality console) meet every spec item. Data Quality state persistence is wired correctly via the existing recommendation_state Mongo collection with item_type='dq', which also correctly prevents user-A's decisions from affecting user-B's view (per-user unique index on user_id+item_type+item_key).
